commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a QQ Bot visible-text sanitizer and wires it through outbound adapter sends, direct gateway dispatch, silent-reply classification, tool-progress text, and official C2C streaming tests.

PR surface: Source +94, Tests +140. Total +234 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main leaves QQ Bot adapter/direct/streaming delivery without a QQ metadata stripper, and the linked issue gives a concrete v2026.6.10 C2C report. I did not run a live QQ Bot account conversation in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-side real QQ Bot behavior proof, not a narrow code repair ClawSweeper can safely produce.

target.title: fix(qqbot): strip echoed inbound metadata from replies
target.body:
Closes #97980

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where QQ Bot users could see OpenClaw's internal inbound metadata at the start of replies, such as `Conversation info (untrusted metadata)` and `Sender (untrusted metadata)` JSON blocks, before the actual assistant response.

## Why This Change Was Made

QQ Bot now runs outbound-visible text through a plugin-owned sanitizer that keeps the existing assistant-visible text cleanup and strips leading echoed inbound metadata blocks before delivery. The same sanitizer is used by the QQ outbound adapter surface, by the direct QQ gateway dispatch path, and by the official C2C streaming path.

The direct QQ gateway path sanitizes block reply text before silent-reply classification as well as before delivery. That keeps metadata-prefixed `NO_REPLY` and `[SKIP]` control replies suppressed instead of stripping the metadata after the silence check and accidentally sending the control token.

The official C2C streaming path still keeps raw text for reply-boundary detection, but sanitizes the normalized visible text before starting or updating a QQ stream. Metadata-only partial updates are held until real visible answer text arrives, so the metadata block is not streamed first.

The change stays local to the QQ Bot plugin and does not alter the prompt envelope sent to the model.

## User Impact

QQ Bot replies no longer expose technical OpenClaw context blocks to end users when a model echoes the inbound prompt metadata. Users see only the intended assistant response text across adapter sends, direct static dispatch, and official C2C streaming, and metadata-prefixed silent replies remain silent.

## Evidence

Duplicate PR gate before implementation:

```text
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --match title,body --limit 20 --json number,title,url,body,updatedAt -- "97980"
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --match title,body --limit 20 --json number,title,url,body,updatedAt -- "内部元数据 渲染 用户聊天框"
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --match title,body --limit 20 --json number,title,url,body,updatedAt -- "qqbot Conversation info Sender untrusted metadata"
```

Observed result: no open PRs matched #97980 or the QQ Bot metadata leak keywords.

Focused tests:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts
# passed: 2 files, 33 tests
```

The focused tests cover:

- QQ Bot outbound adapter sanitizer strips raw/fenced inbound metadata blocks.
- Direct gateway dispatch strips metadata from a normal block reply before QQ delivery.
- Direct gateway dispatch keeps metadata-prefixed `NO_REPLY` suppressed.
- Official C2C streaming does not send metadata-only partial chunks and streams only `final answer` once visible answer text arrives.

Formatting, diff hygiene, and local review:

```text
corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/qqbot/src/engine/utils/visible-text.ts extensions/qqbot/src/channel.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/streaming-c2c.ts extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts
# passed

git diff --check
# passed

.agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local
# passed with no reported output
```

Real sanitizer proof using the production QQ Bot visible-text helper with the issue's metadata shape:

```text
node --import tsx --input-type=module
# input: leading Conversation info + Sender JSON metadata blocks followed by "final answer"
# output: { "sanitized": "final answer" }
```

Follow-up after ClawSweeper review:

```text
# ClawSweeper reported that metadata-prefixed NO_REPLY/[SKIP] could be stripped after the silent check.
# Fixed by sanitizing block reply text before silent classification and delivery planning.
# Added regression coverage for metadata-prefixed NO_REPLY remaining suppressed.
# Also sanitized the official C2C streaming visible text path and added coverage that metadata-only partial replies are not streamed.
```

Not tested: a live QQ Bot account conversation. The proof covers the plugin outbound adapter sanitizer, direct gateway dispatch, and official C2C streaming path with focused tests, but no redacted live QQ Bot output, log, screenshot, or recording was available from this environment.
